hypothesis: GGD binding becomes more deterministic if it must declare a repo-local lawset index and binding verification fails whenever that index is missing, empty, or points at missing lawset files.
measurement: Before the ratchet, the control-plane brief could cite `GGD/equations/lawsets.json` as the first repo-local mathematical surface even though the file was absent and `node CLAW/scripts/verify-ggd-binding.mjs` still verified clean because it never required or inspected a local lawset index. After the ratchet, `GGD/project.binding.json` pins `system_optimizer.local_lawset_index`, the repo carries `GGD/equations/lawsets.json`, and the binding verifier rejects missing, empty, duplicate-id, or missing-path lawset index entries while the fixed scope-local promotion bundle stays green.
keep_rule: Keep only if `node CLAW/scripts/verify-ggd-binding.mjs` now fails a missing or malformed repo-local lawset index contract and the exact five-command scope-local promotion bundle passes without touching route implementation paths.
learning_item: The GGD binding must expose a repo-local lawset index as a first-class contract surface; relying only on external example-lawset pointers leaves equation truth partly implicit and lets missing local registry surfaces verify clean.
